nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic AilГ©ne, AilГӯn, from ail вҖҳrockвҖҷ. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bИі вҖҳvillageвҖҷ, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746вҖ“1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (AlГ©n): habitational name from one of the many places called AlГ©n in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alГ©n вҖҳbeyondвҖҷ.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal вҖҳnobleвҖҷ + haid вҖҳвҖҳrank, worthвҖҷвҖҷ.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al вҖҳalderвҖҷ.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names В© Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: вҖҳGod is graciousвҖҷ. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Brockhouse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Brookhouse  .
Americanized form of German   Brockhaus  .

variants.name: Brookhouse
meanings: English: topographic name for a house by a stream, from Middle English brok(e) вҖҳbrookвҖҷ + hous вҖҳhouseвҖҷ, or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Brookhouse in Laughton (Yorkshire).
Americanized form of German   Brockhaus  .
Americanized form of Dutch   Broekhuis  .

en.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen was born in 1356, in England, United Kingdom as the son of Richard Allen. He had at least 1 son with Alice Maschon. He died in 1430, in his hometown, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen est nГ© en 1356, Г  Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Richard Allen. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Alice Maschon. Il est dГ©cГ©dГ© en 1430, dans sa ville natale, Г  lвҖҷГўge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen wurde 1356 in England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Richard Allen geboren. Er hatte mit Alice Maschon mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1430 im Alter von 74 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen ГЁ nato il nel 1356 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Richard Allen. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Alice Maschon. MorГ¬ nel 1430, nel sua cittadina, all'etГ  di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen(мқҖ)лҠ” 1356л…„ мҳҒкөӯ мһүкёҖлһңл“ңм—җм„ң Richard Allenмқҳ м•„л“ӨлЎң нғңм–ҙлӮ¬лӢӨ. к·ёлҠ” Alice Maschon(мҷҖ)кіј м Ғм–ҙлҸ„ н•ң лӘ…мқҳ м•„л“Өмқ„ л‘җм—ҲлӢӨ. к·ёлҠ” 1430л…„ к·ёмқҳ кі н–Ҙм—җм„ң 74м„ёмқҳ лӮҳмқҙлЎң мӮ¬л§қн–ҲлӢӨ.

pt.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen nasceu em 1356, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Richard Allen. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Alice Maschon. Ele faleceu em 1430, em sua cidade natal, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen СҖРҫРҙРёР»СҒСҸ РІ 1356 Рі. РІ РјРөСҒСӮРҪРҫСҒСӮРё РҗРҪРіР»РёСҸ, Р’РөР»РёРәРҫРұСҖРёСӮР°РҪРёСҸ. Р•РіРҫ РҫСӮСҶР° Р·РІР°Р»Рё Richard Allen. РЈ РҪРөРіРҫ РұСӢР» РҝРҫ РәСҖР°Р№РҪРөР№ РјРөСҖРө 1 СҒСӢРҪ РҫСӮ Alice Maschon. РһРҪ СғРјРөСҖ РІ 1430 Рі. РІ РөРіРҫ СҖРҫРҙРҪРҫРј РіРҫСҖРҫРҙРө РІ РІРҫР·СҖР°СҒСӮРө 74 Р»РөСӮ.

es.lifeSummary: John Brockhouse Allen naciГі en 1356, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Richard Allen. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Alice Maschon. MuriГі en 1430, en su pueblo, a la edad de 74 aГұos.
